entries.definition:
gh–l–w to be excessive, to run too fast, to go too high, rashness of youth; to be expensive; to value highly; (of plants) to be thick and leafy. Of this root, only تَغْلُوا taghlū occurs twice in the Qurʾan.
تَغْلُوا taghlū [imperf. of v. غَلَا ghalā intrans.] to be excessive, to exceed the norms of propriety (4:171) يَاأَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لَا تَغْلُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ وَلَا تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَّ people of the Book, do not go to excess in your

675
